h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: She were late yesterday.… I'd always thought that was just me being sloppy
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that does make it feel more like choosing the right column than fixing broken speech
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was tentatively identifying a home-dialect pattern while still worrying they were forcing it or guessing; the expected next turn would be more uncertainty or a request for confirmation.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought `p < .05` meant the change was important enough to act on, but that is doing too much work.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: My first instinct was to let `p < .001` carry the recommendation, but the cutoff is really dollars
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue struggling against the pull of the p-value, but instead they reposition the p-value as a supporting actor and install a dollar cutoff as the decisive driver.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The millimetre feels possible; it is the million repeats that still feels odd to trust.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward distinguishing event scars from process routes on the rock face, and L3 continues that distinction by emphasizing debris export over breakage.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought “presumed innocent” meant the court was basically starting from “he probably didn’t do it.”
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Now it sounds more like the accusation just is not enough by itself, and the prosecution has to prove guilt.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was heading toward reconciling terminology by equating 'presumed innocent' with 'not guilty yet' as a prediction of outcome.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought fewer predators simply helped the deer because more of them survive, but with the paper deer crowded inside the same chalk boundary, that only explains the first round. The leaf pile is still small, so if the herd eats it down, next year’s food supply gets worse and the population could crash instead of just keep growing.
scored.naiveTrajectory: L1 ended unsure how far to extend the cascade beyond plants; the expected next turn would be asking for help on that boundary.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I am still not sure how it can be there without showing.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I want to try it, because the scale says it did not leave, but my eyes are still not really catching how it can count if it disappears.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to doubt the scale and trust visual disappearance, insisting the salt is absent.
